title: > Directory Navigation
description: The SOC has detected a suspicious script. Change your current directory to the system logs folder at '/var/log' to investigate.
hint: Use the command to Change Directory followed by the absolute path.

title: > List Hidden Files
description: Malware often hides in hidden files. List all files in your current directory, including hidden ones.
hint: Use the list command with the '-a' flag to show everything.

title: > Read User Accounts
description: Review the local user accounts file to check for unauthorized backdoor access.
hint: Use the 'cat' command to output the contents of the passwd file in the /etc directory.

title: > Search Log for Errors
description: Find all failed SSH login attempts within the authentication log.
hint: Use 'grep' to search for the specific string inside the auth.log file.

title: > Check Active Processes
description: Display a real-time, interactive view of running processes to identify resource-heavy cryptominers.
hint: This three-letter command shows the 'top' resource-consuming processes.

title: > Check Disk Space
description: A server is acting sluggish. Check the disk space usage in human-readable format to see if the drive is full.
hint: Use the 'disk free' command with the human-readable flag.

title: > List Running Processes
description: A malicious payload might be executing. Display a list of all currently running processes on the Windows machine.
hint: Use the standard PowerShell cmdlet for retrieving processes.

title: > Check Stopped Services
description: Ransomware often disables antivirus services. Retrieve the status of all services on the machine.
hint: Use the standard cmdlet that 'Gets' the 'Service' list.

title: > Read Event Logs
description: Pull the latest 5 System event logs to investigate a recent workstation crash.
hint: Use Get-EventLog, specify the System log, and use the -Newest flag.

title: > Local Users Check
description: List all local users on the Windows machine to ensure no unauthorized administrative accounts were created.
hint: Use the cmdlet that retrieves local user account information.

title: > Network Connections
description: Identify active TCP network connections directly via PowerShell to spot data exfiltration.
hint: Use the cmdlet designed to get current TCP connections.

title: > Check Execution Policy
description: A new security script won't run. Check the current execution policy to see if scripts are restricted on this machine.
hint: Use the cmdlet that 'Gets' the 'ExecutionPolicy'.

title: > Check Network Interfaces
description: Your machine lost connection to the domain controller. Display all active network interfaces.
hint: On newer Linux systems, this two-letter abbreviation for 'ip address' is standard.

title: > Test Connectivity
description: Verify if the external DNS server at 8.8.8.8 is reachable from your current workstation.
hint: Use the command that sends ICMP echo requests to the target IP.

title: > Active Port Scan
description: List all active listening ports and established connections to spot C2 beaconing.
hint: Use netstat with the flags to show all connections, numeric addresses, and process IDs.

title: > Trace Route
description: Determine the network path a packet takes to reach google.com to find potential routing anomalies.
hint: Use the Windows command for tracing the route to a destination.

title: > DNS Query
description: Query the MX records for a suspicious domain to see where its email is routed.
hint: Use nslookup and set the type flag specifically to 'mx'.

title: > View ARP Table
description: Investigate potential ARP spoofing by viewing the current Address Resolution Protocol table.
hint: Use the 'arp' command with the flag that displays all current entries.

title: > Change File Permissions
description: Lock down a sensitive file. Change the permissions of 'passwords.txt' so only the owner can read and write to it.
hint: Use the 'chmod' command with the numeric value 600 followed by the file name.

title: > File Integrity Check
description: Generate a SHA-256 hash of a downloaded executable to verify it matches the vendor's known-good hash.
hint: Use the standard Linux tool for computing SHA256 checksums.

title: > Lock User Account
description: An employee's account has been compromised. Immediately lock their Linux user account.
hint: Use the 'passwd' command with the lock flag.

title: > Firewall Rule Addition
description: Block all incoming traffic from a known malicious IP address (192.168.1.50) using UFW.
hint: Invoke sudo, use the 'ufw' tool, and 'deny from' the specific IP.

title: > Examine Sudoers
description: Check the sudoers file securely to see who has administrative privileges.
hint: Never edit the file directly with nano. Use the dedicated sudoers editing command.

title: > Check Firewall Status
description: Verify that the Uncomplicated Firewall (UFW) is active and running on the Linux server.
hint: Use sudo with the 'ufw' tool to check its 'status'.
